name: Sahagún from León – circular via Melgar de Abajo and río Cea
name_original: Sahagún desde León – circular por Melgar de Abajo y río Cea
region_intro: Road cycling routes around León offer diverse terrain, from the flat plains of the Páramo Leonés to the challenging mountain passes near the Picos de Europa. The region is characterized by river basins such as the Bernesga, Torío, and Curueño, which often feature in scenic routes. Cyclists can find routes across vast arable farmland or through areas with more significant elevation changes, providing varied experiences. Best road cycling routes around León The most popular road cycling route is La Robla from León – circular by the Torío and Bernesga rivers, a 44.0 miles (70.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This circular route follows the Torío and Bernesga rivers. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villanueva del Árbol from Leon – circular of the Torío River, an easy 21.7 miles (34.9 km) path. This route offers a circular ride along the Torío River. Local road cyclists also love the Villarroañe from León – circular by Puente Villarente, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail leading through the plains and past Puente Villarente, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Road cycling around León is defined by flat plains, river basins, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easier rides on well-paved surfaces to demanding ascents. The routes in León are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore León's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around León?
A: León offers a wide variety of road cycling experiences, with over 100 routes available. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy rides to challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty levels can I expect on road cycling routes in León?
A: The León region provides diverse terrain for road cyclists. You'll find routes across the flat plains of the Páramo Leonés, scenic rides along river basins like the Torío and Bernesga, and challenging mountain passes as you approach the Picos de Europa. There are 26 easy routes, 76 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes to choose from.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in the León region?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find demanding ascents and challenging routes, particularly in areas closer to the Picos de Europa mountain range. The region is known for its 'legendary climbs' associated with La Vuelta a España, offering thrilling challenges. There are 16 difficult routes available for those seeking a strenuous ride.

Q: Can beginners or families find easy road cycling routes around León?
A: Absolutely. León offers 26 easy road cycling routes, many of which follow well-paved surfaces and feature minimal elevation changes, such as those found in the Páramo Leonés. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046644656/villanueva-del-arbol-from-leon-circular-of-the-torio-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva del Árbol from Leon – circular of the Torío River&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21.7-mile path.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes available from León?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around León are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046667297/la-robla-from-leon-circular-by-the-torio-and-bernesga-rivers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Robla from León – circular by the Torío and Bernesga rivers&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046653873/villarroane-from-leon-circular-by-puente-villarente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarroañe from León – circular by Puente Villarente&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see while road cycling near León?
A: León's road cycling routes often pass by significant historical and cultural sites. Within the city, you can explore the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5658871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman City Walls&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum&lt;/a&gt;. Routes along the Camino de Santiago also feature historic towns and medieval bridges.

Q: What natural features and landscapes will I encounter on road cycling routes in León?
A: You'll experience a variety of natural beauty, from the expansive arable farmland of the Páramo Leonés to the picturesque river basins of the Bernesga, Torío, and Curueño. Further afield, the routes offer stunning views of the Montaña de Riaño and the dramatic landscapes of the Picos de Europa.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in León?
A: While winters can be cold with snow, the relatively cool temperatures in summer make León an attractive destination for cycling compared to many other inland Spanish cities. Spring and autumn also offer pleasant conditions for exploring the region's diverse routes.

Q: Can I cycle a section of the Camino de Santiago on a road bike from León?
A: Yes, León is a crucial stop on the Camino Frances route of the Camino de Santiago. You can cycle sections of this iconic pilgrimage, transitioning from flatter plains to green hills and charming villages, offering a unique and mythical cycling experience. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046665277/leon-camino-frances-loop-via-carrizo-de-la-ribera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León - Camino Francés Loop via Carrizo de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; for a taste of this journey.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in León?
A: The road cycling routes around León are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from flat, fast sections to challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore both natural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling from León?
A: León is part of a larger network of long-distance cycle-tourism routes within Castilla y León, connecting provincial capitals and rural areas. The EuroVelo 3 (Pilgrim Route) also passes through this region, providing opportunities for extended journeys. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046635972/santa-maria-del-paramo-from-leon-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria del Paramo from Leon – circular&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial ride of over 80 kilometers.
tips: Without a doubt, the Cathedral is the emblem of the city and its history begins in the 10th century. On top of the old baths and public buildings that the Legio VII had built in the middle of the 2nd century, stood the royal palace of Ordoño II. In 917, after defeating the Arabs in the battle of San Esteban de Gormaz, the king gave up his residence "gratefully to God" so that the first cathedral temple was erected there.
Impressive old masonry bridge over the Torío River crossed by those who make the Camino de Santiago.
Ruins of fortifications and walls around the old town of Mansilla de las Mulas on the banks of the Esla river. The town is located on the Camino de Santiago Francés.
Rest area with picnic areas and water fountain.

Sahagún is a regional town in the province of Leon located between the Cea and Valderaduey rivers. A center for commerce already during the Roman occupation of the Iberian peninsula, it gained importance by being along the Jacobian pilgrim route towards Santiago.

A small rural town in Valladolid that borders the province of León on the Cea River. The most notable buildings in the town are its two churches. The church of San Juan from the 18th century and the church of El Salvador from the 16th century.
